name: Saad ibn Hanzala
title: Lion of Tamim
shortDescription: A brave warrior from the Tamim tribe who went to the battlefield reciting epic poetry and was martyred courageously.
fullStory:
Sa'd ibn Hanzala al-Taymi was a brave horseman and warrior of the Bani Tamim tribe. He joined the Master of Martyrs' (AS) caravan in Mecca or along the way.

On Ashura, he was one of those who spared no effort to defend the Imam and the Ahl al-Bayt's sanctuary. When his turn came, he entered the battlefield with courage and firmness.

He recited epic poetry on the battlefield, saying: 'The sword is in my right hand, and I defend Hussain (AS) with spear and sword...'

After a heavy fight and striking down a number of enemy soldiers, Sa'd attained the great grace of martyrdom with a body full of wounds and joined the saved ones of history.
quote: (His Rajaz in battle:) We practice patience in the face of swords and spears to reach the highest paradise...
